No layout loaded. Load one in Setup to see the primary mission and objectives.

Secondary objectives

Draw from the Tactical deck. Drawn cards are shared — your opponent sees your hand too. Tap 📖 to read a card; card text is editable so you can match your own deck exactly.

Set up the profile and roll. Full dice detail is logged for both players.

Battle-shock / Leadership

You

Battlefield

Terrain & objectives

Layouts are symmetric tournament-style maps scaled to your board. Or place by hand: pick a tool in the toolbar, drag to place terrain, click to drop objectives. Select + R rotates, Del removes.

Rules aids

Dragging models shows the distance moved vs the unit's Move stat — white within M, yellow up to M+6" (advance territory), red beyond. Your opponent sees your drag path live.
Models breaking coherency (2" of a squadmate, 9" of every squadmate) glow red. With enforcement on, a drag that breaks a previously-coherent unit is reverted.

Game

Unit card
ARMY BUILDER

Roster

Roster auto-saves in your browser. Detachments and enhancements aren't validated — check your faction pack.

Import card text

Paste your cards' text here to load your whole deck at once — it saves locally and shares to your opponent. Use one block per card, each starting with ## Card Name on its own line, followed by that card's text. (Single lines like Card Name :: text also work.)

A brief summary of what this mission rewards. The exact scoring is on your physical card — paste it here to keep it handy, and it'll show for both players.

Secondary objective deck

One card name per line — edit to match the deck you own. Only card names are stored; keep your physical cards for the scoring rules.

Import army list

Paste an army list (official app export or similar). I'll detect the faction, match every unit to the database, and build your cards — stats, weapons, bases and model counts included.

How to play online

  1. One of you clicks Host and reads out the room code (also copied to your clipboard).
  2. The other opens this same file and clicks Join, entering the code. You each need this HTML file — just send it over.
  3. Set your name and side in Setup. Everything on the table, the trackers, dice and the log are shared live.

The table

  • Select tool: drag models to move them. Drag on empty space for a box-select; drag any selected model to move the group. Del removes, R rotates vehicles/terrain.
  • With models selected, press 1–4 to toggle range rings (6"/9"/12"/24"). Click a model to see its unit; use ± on the unit card to track wounds.
  • Squad leaders (sergeants, nobs, superiors…) deploy with a gold ring automatically. Select any model and press W to give it a short weapon tag (e.g. "PK", "RL", "MLT") shown under its base — mark your special weapons so both players can see who's carrying what.
  • Tape measure: drag to measure in inches — your opponent sees your measurement too.
  • Builder (full screen): build a points-legal roster — pick faction and points limit, click unit cards to add them, change sizes, watch the total. Muster army puts every unit on the table in one click and returns you to the game. Esc closes it. Export the list as text to share it.
  • Army tab: every faction's units are pre-loaded — pick your faction, click a unit to add its card (stats, weapons, bases, points), or Import army list to paste a list and get your whole army on the table in one go. Cards save in your browser.
  • Coherency: models breaking unit coherency glow red and moves that break a coherent unit snap back (toggle in Setup).
  • Terrain layouts: Setup tab → pick a tournament-style layout → Load. Objectives included.
  • Cards tab: a shared scoreboard (VP/CP/round, synced with the top bar), the current primary mission, and a Tactical secondary deck you draw from — both players see each other's hands. Tap 📖 on any card to read its summary; the text is editable so you can paste the exact scoring from the deck you own, and it syncs to your opponent.
  • Attack tab: enter the weapon profile and target; it rolls the whole 11th-edition sequence (crits, Sustained, Lethal, Dev Wounds, Anti-X, Twin-linked, invulns, FNP) and logs the dice for both players.
  • Save game (Setup tab) downloads the whole table state; either player can reload it later.

Rules note: you and your opponent apply the game rules — this table doesn't police them, it just does the rolling and bookkeeping. Points and datasheets: official app / Munitorum Field Manual.
